summary: This is an OVARIA IPT (Intellectual Property Team) update from AthenaDAO describing a Menopause Risk Model (MRM) project. Gero AI developed machine learning models to predict age at natural menopause using UK Biobank data, correcting for self-reporting biases. The team conducted GWAS and Mendelian Randomization analysis identifying genetic links between menopause timing and disease risk, finding potential causal relationships with osteoporosis and endometriosis.

content_blocks.title: Menopause Risk Model (MRM) - Problem Statement
content_blocks.content: Age at natural menopause (ANM) data are self-reported and prone to recall distortion or 'telescoping' bias. Gero AI modeled ANM in UK Biobank women to correct for these biases and covariates including BMI, smoking, and deprivation index.

content_blocks.title: Machine Learning Model Development
content_blocks.content: Gero tested multiple ML approaches: (1) Gradient-boosted trees achieved the highest accuracy, (2) The accelerated failure Weibull model reached similar performance but was far more interpretable. These models were then used to run GWAS (Genome-Wide Association Studies) and Mendelian Randomization to link menopause timing with genetics and disease risk.

content_blocks.title: Key Findings
content_blocks.content: Three major findings from the analysis: (1) Earlier menopause correlates with >100 diseases observationally, (2) True causal effects may exist with osteoporosis and endometriosis specifically, (3) Targets replicated across various datasets, suggesting robustness of findings.

key_findings: Computational modeling project using UK Biobank data - large-scale population genomics dataset
Machine learning models developed: gradient-boosted trees (highest accuracy) and accelerated failure Weibull model (interpretable)
GWAS and Mendelian Randomization analysis completed - genetic validation approach
Observational correlations identified with >100 diseases linked to earlier menopause
Potential causal relationships identified with osteoporosis and endometriosis through Mendelian Randomization
Target validation across multiple datasets demonstrating replication
Project appears to be at discovery/target identification stage (TRL 1-2) - computational analysis identifying disease associations but no therapeutic intervention described
UK Biobank provides large sample size for statistical power but no experimental validation in disease models mentioned
